$comment: Beats for the ~1-min Stardew Clawd intro video. Feature-focused tour — no technique bragging, no proud-of section. Each beat: TTS text + an action key the orchestrator runs WHILE the audio plays. A global ambient ticker fires random tool activity on all workers throughout, so the office is never visually still.
instructions: A warm, delighted, passionate male voice — like someone showing off a project they genuinely love. Friendly and energetic, never theatrical. Smile through the words; let the wonder come through. Pronounce 'Clawd' exactly like the name 'Claude' (rhymes with 'lord'), because that's the joke — it's a Claude pun. Even, confident pace with subtle warmth on the product name 'Stardew Clawd'. Slight pauses at periods.

beats.text: Claude Code sessions are invisible — you start one, and you wait. Stardew Clawd turns every session into a pixel-art worker, sitting at a desk in a cozy office.
When Claude reads a file, your worker reads. When it edits code, they type. When it runs a command, they bash on the keyboard.
When Claude needs your input, a sign pops up over the worker's head. Click them to answer right there.
Your answer flows back, and the session continues. No terminal switching.
Hire more workers and the office grows — multiple rooms, drag to pan, slider to zoom.
Don't like the cozy farm look? Swap scenes. Modern office, school, lab, construction site — same workers, totally different vibe.
That's Stardew Clawd. Pixel-art workers for your Claude Code sessions.
